title: Chocolate chip cookie bars
subtitle: Thick, chewy chocolate chip cookies baked as a slab
excerpt: Chocolate chip cookie dough pressed into a tin and baked as a single thick slab, then cut into bars once cool. The method produces a chewier, more fudgy result than individual cookies: the centre stays soft and the edges set to a slight crunch, and the thick format means more of the cross-section is the tender middle that most people prefer. Quicker to make than dropping and spacing individual cookies.
sourceNotes: Chocolate chip cookies baked as a slab or bar are documented in American baking literature as a practical variation of the standard drop cookie, appearing in American home economics and cookery books from the mid-twentieth century onward. The drop cookie itself originates with Ruth Wakefield, Toll House Tried and True Recipes (1938, now in the public domain under US copyright duration rules). The bar format is described in American domestic baking guides from the 1940s.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cut into bars and freeze individually wrapped. Defrost at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The unbaked dough can be pressed into the tin, covered, and refrigerated for up to 2 days before baking. Bake from cold, adding 3 to 4 minutes to the bake time.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 160°C. The bars are done when the top is golden and set at the edges but still looks slightly underdone in the centre; they firm on cooling.

recipeTools.notes: 23 x 33 cm tin, lined with baking paper.

glossaryTerms.term: Pressing cookie dough into a tin
glossaryTerms.definition: Distributing thick cookie dough evenly into a lined baking tin before baking. The dough will be stiff; use lightly dampened hands or the base of a measuring cup to press it into an even layer, reaching into the corners without leaving gaps or thin spots. An uneven layer bakes unevenly: thin corners overcook before the centre sets. Pressing firmly ensures the bars cut cleanly rather than crumbling at the edges when sliced.

attrs.term: Pressing cookie dough into a tin

content.text: pressing the stiff dough evenly into the tin
so it bakes uniformly. The bars need to cool completely before cutting: warm, they will fall apart at the cut. Chill for 15 minutes before slicing if you want very clean edges.

Heat the oven to 180°C conventional (160°C fan). Line a 23 x 33 cm tin with baking paper. Whisk the melted butter, caster sugar, and dark brown sugar together in a large bowl until combined. Beat in the eggs and vanilla. Stir in the flour, bicarbonate of soda, and salt until just combined. Fold in the chocolate chips.

Tip the dough into the prepared tin and press firmly into an even layer with lightly dampened hands. Bake for 22 to 26 minutes until the top is golden and set at the edges and the centre looks slightly underdone. Cool completely in the tin before lifting out and cutting into bars. For the cleanest cuts, refrigerate for 15 minutes before slicing.
